title: Dentist
description: Dentists are healthcare professionals who diagnose, treat, and prevent diseases and conditions of the oral cavity. They promote oral health through education and various dental procedures.
content: Dentists require a strong foundation in science, excellent manual dexterity, and communication skills. They diagnose oral health issues, perform treatments like fillings, root canals, extractions, and cosmetic procedures. Many dentists in India run their own private practices, while others work in hospitals, clinics, or government health services. Staying updated with the latest advancements in dentistry is crucial for providing quality care.

financialFeasibility.note: Dental education is relatively expensive in India. While government colleges offer subsidized fees, private colleges can be a significant financial burden. Loan options are available, but financial planning is essential.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private sectors. Private practice is a common and potentially lucrative path, while government jobs offer stability and benefits.

relatedCareers.reason: Works under the supervision of a dentist to provide preventative oral care.
relatedCareers.title: Dental Hygienist

relatedCareers.reason: Specializes in correcting teeth and jaw alignment.
relatedCareers.title: Orthodontist

relatedCareers.reason: Performs surgical procedures on the mouth, jaws, and face.
relatedCareers.title: Oral Surgeon

genderDistribution.note: While dentistry was traditionally male-dominated, the number of female dentists is steadily increasing in India. Societal biases are diminishing, and both genders are equally capable.
